title: blumea obliqua var. aurita
intro: Blumea obliqua var. aurita is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Himalayan region, also known as the 'Fairy's Helmet' or 'Fairy's Head.' It is a type of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Blumea obliqua var. aurita include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
